metadata.description: MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped to KQL detection examples for Microsoft Sentinel and Defender XDR
metadata.caveat: KQL examples are templates for learning; production queries should include additional tuning, exclusions, and threshold testing

kql_detections.kql_example: EmailEvents | where Subject contains "Urgent" and Subject contains "Verify" and FileName has_any (".zip", ".exe", ".scr") | summarize count() by SenderFromAddress, Subject, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: May produce false positives on legitimate urgent emails; refine with sender domain reputation and URL inspection.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("powershell -enc", "cmd.exe /c", "powershell.exe -nop") and InitiatingProcessFileName != "explorer.exe" | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: High entropy obfuscation may evade simple string matching; use YARA rules or ML-based detection for advanced evasion.

kql_detections.kql_example: AuditLogs | where OperationName == "Add member to group" and TargetResources[0].displayName has "admin" | extend UserId = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.id) | project Timestamp, UserId, OperationName, TargetResources
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate admins add members frequently; requires baseline of normal admin activity and comparison to detect anomalies.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceRegistryEvents | where RegistryKey contains_any ("Run", "RunOnce", "Services") and RegistryValueName !in ("Windows Defender", "Windows Update") | project DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Many legitimate apps use Run/RunOnce; whitelist known-good processes and monitor for anomalous binary paths.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("runas /user:", "UAC", "DLL hijacking") or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in ("consent.exe", "eventviewer.exe") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: UAC bypass techniques vary; correlate with Defender alerts and device isolation events for confirmation.

kql_detections.kql_example: SigninLogs | where ResultType == "50058" or ResultType == "50076" | summarize FailedAttempts = count() by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, 5m) | where FailedAttempts > 5
kql_detections.kql_caveat: May trigger on users with fat-finger password errors; correlate with threat intelligence and impossible travel to increase confidence.

kql_detections.kql_example: AuditLogs | where OperationName has_any ("List users", "Get user", "Enumerate members") | summarize Discoveries = count() by InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName, bin(Timestamp, 10m) | where Discoveries > 20
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Help desk and service account enumeration is normal; baseline legitimate discovery patterns to detect anomalies.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemotePort in (3389, 22, 5985, 445) and InitiatingProcessName !in ("explorer.exe", "svchost.exe") | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: RDP and SSH are legitimate for admin activity; filter by business hours, geography, and known bastion hosts.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where Protocol == "http" and RemoteIP != "192.168.1.0/24" | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ActionType, Timestamp | where ActionType == "InboundConnectionAccepted"
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Requires network context; combine with DNS sinkhole data and TLS certificate mismatches for higher confidence.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteIP not in ("trusted_ip_range") and TotalBytesSent > TotalBytesReceived * 5 | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, TotalBytesSent, InitiatingProcessName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate backup and sync tools send large amounts of data; establish baseline data transfer patterns per device and application.

kql_detections.kql_example: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 5156 and DestinationPort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443) and Application not contains "Chrome" and Application not contains "Firefox" | project Computer, Application, Destination IP, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Many legitimate apps use HTTP/HTTPS; correlate with certificate analysis, YARA rules, and payload inspection.

kql_detections.kql_example: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4689 and CommandLine contains_any ("taskkill /im defender", "Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring") | project Computer, CommandLine, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Noisy; filter on non-admin processes and correlate with other suspicious activity.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("certutil -decode", "base64 -d", "powershell -enc") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate IT operations use certutil for certificates; require unusual script hosts or suspicious payloads.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceFileEvents | where FileName in ("icacls.exe", "takeown.exe") and InitiatingProcessParentFileName !in ("explorer.exe", "cmd.exe") | project DeviceName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: System administrators frequently modify permissions; require non-admin context or unusual target directories.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("lsass.exe", "mimikatz", "hashdump", "Get-Process lsass") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate forensic tools may trigger false positives; require correlation with other persistence/lateral movement indicators.

kql_detections.kql_example: SecurityAlert | where Title contains "Phishing" or Title contains "BEC" | project Title, AlertName, ResourceIdentifiers, Timestamp | where Timestamp > ago(1h)
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Sentinel alerts vary in quality; manually validate and establish feedback loops with email team.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("keylog", "key?logger", "inputremapping") or FileName in ("iexplore.exe", "chrome.exe") and InitiatingProcessFileName in ("powershell.exe", "cmd.exe") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: High false positive rate; correlate with malware hash, certificate signing, and installation sources.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemotePort in (445, 3389, 5985) and ActionType == "InboundConnectionAccepted" and RemoteIP not in ("corp_bastion_ips") | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Requires network baseline; combine with vulnerability scan data to identify unpatched systems.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceFileEvents | where FileName in ("mimikatz.exe", "procdump.exe", "psexec.exe") and InitiatingProcessParentFileName !in ("explorer.exe", "svchost.exe") | project DeviceName, FileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: False positives from legitimate pen testing; require correlation with C2 beacons or credential theft indicators.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine contains_any ("mciSendString", "waveOutOpen", "audioapis") and InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("skype.exe", "teams.exe", "zoom.exe") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Many legitimate communication apps access audio; filter by unexpected process names and paths.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where TotalBytesSent > 1000000 and RemoteIP != "internal_ip_range" and InitiatingProcessFileName not in ("outlook.exe", "teams.exe") | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, TotalBytesSent, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate cloud sync and backup tools transfer large volumes; baseline by application and user.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceNetworkEvents | where Protocol == "udp" and RemotePort in (53, 123, 161) and TotalBytesSent > TotalBytesReceived * 3 | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, Protocol, TotalBytesSent, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: DNS and NTP carry legitimate traffic; requires pattern analysis and payload inspection to detect exfiltration.

kql_detections.tactic: Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion
kql_detections.kql_example: SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0 and UserPrincipalName contains "@" and LocationDetails.countryOrRegion != "US" | summarize SigninCount = count() by UserPrincipalName, LocationDetails.countryOrRegion, bin(Timestamp, 1h) | where SigninCount > 5
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Legitimate travel and VPN use may trigger; establish geographic baselines per user.

kql_detections.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution - Winlogon Helper DLL
kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceRegistryEvents | where RegistryKey contains "\\Control Panel\\Lsa" and (RegistryValueName contains "Notification Packages" or RegistryValueName contains "AppSetup") | project DeviceName, RegistryValueData, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Rare technique; correlate with file creation in System32 and unsigned DLL analysis.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceFileEvents | where FileName endswith_any (".locked", ".encrypted", ".conti", ".lockbit", ".ransom") | summarize EncryptedCount = count() by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, bin(Timestamp, 5m) | where EncryptedCount > 10
kql_detections.kql_caveat: High signal quality; immediately isolate device and preserve forensics.

kql_detections.kql_example: SecurityEvent | where EventID == 4688 and CommandLine contains_any ("vssadmin delete shadows", "cipher /w:", "wmic shadowcopy delete") | project Computer, CommandLine, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: Highly indicative of ransomware; immediate response recommended.

kql_detections.kql_example: DeviceFileEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName in ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash") and FileName endswith_any (".docx", ".pptx", ".xlsx", ".pdf") | project DeviceName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, Timestamp
kql_detections.kql_caveat: High false positive rate from normal user work; correlate with exfiltration events to confirm intent.
